diseases.name: Acute Myocardial Infarction
diseases.description: Acute myocardial infarction (AMI), commonly known as a heart attack, occurs when blood flow to a portion of the heart muscle is severely reduced or interrupted, resulting in necrosis of the myocardial tissue. It is a medical emergency requiring immediate intervention.
common_symptoms: Radiating pain to left arm, jaw, or back
Anxiety or sense of impending doom
risk_factors: Family history of coronary artery disease
treatment_protocols: Thrombolytic therapy if PCI unavailable within 120 minutes

severity_levels.mild: NSTEMI with minor troponin elevation, hemodynamically stable
severity_levels.moderate: NSTEMI with significant troponin elevation or STEMI with single vessel involvement
severity_levels.severe: STEMI with cardiogenic shock, multi-vessel disease, or cardiac arrest

diseases.name: Community-Acquired Pneumonia
diseases.description: Community-acquired pneumonia (CAP) is an acute infection of the pulmonary parenchyma acquired outside of a healthcare setting. It is one of the most common infectious diseases and a leading cause of morbidity and mortality worldwide, particularly in the elderly and immunocompromised.
treatment_protocols: CURB-65 or PSI scoring for severity assessment
Outpatient: Respiratory fluoroquinolone or beta-lactam + macrolide (comorbidities)
Inpatient non-ICU: Beta-lactam + macrolide or respiratory fluoroquinolone

severity_levels.severe: CURB-65 score 3-5, ICU admission, possible mechanical ventilation

diseases.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
diseases.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus is a chronic metabolic disorder characterized by insulin resistance and progressive beta-cell dysfunction, leading to hyperglycemia. It is the most common form of diabetes, accounting for approximately 90-95% of all diabetes cases, and is associated with significant long-term microvascular and macrovascular complications.
risk_factors: Ethnic predisposition (African American, Hispanic, Native American)
treatment_protocols: GLP-1 receptor agonist or SGLT2 inhibitor (high cardiovascular/renal risk)
DPP-4 inhibitor or sulfonylurea as add-on
Basal insulin if HbA1c >10% or symptomatic hyperglycemia

severity_levels.mild: HbA1c <7.5%, no complications, manageable with lifestyle and monotherapy
severity_levels.moderate: HbA1c 7.5-9.0%, early microvascular complications, dual/triple therapy required
severity_levels.severe: HbA1c >9.0%, established complications, insulin-dependent, or diabetic emergencies

diseases.name: Ischemic Stroke
diseases.description: Ischemic stroke occurs when blood supply to part of the brain is interrupted or reduced, preventing brain tissue from receiving oxygen and nutrients. It accounts for approximately 87% of all strokes and is a leading cause of long-term disability. Time-sensitive interventions are critical for optimal outcomes.
recommended_tests: CT Angiography (CTA) of head and neck
treatment_protocols: IV Alteplase (tPA) within 4.5 hours of symptom onset
Mechanical thrombectomy for large vessel occlusion within 24 hours
Antiplatelet therapy (aspirin 160-325mg) after 24h if tPA given
Blood pressure management (permissive hypertension if not receiving tPA)

severity_levels.mild: NIHSS score 1-4, small vessel lacunar infarct, good functional outcome expected
severity_levels.moderate: NIHSS score 5-15, cortical involvement, some residual deficit likely
severity_levels.severe: NIHSS score >15, large vessel occlusion, significant disability or mortality risk

diseases.name: Congestive Heart Failure
diseases.description: Congestive heart failure (CHF) is a clinical syndrome in which the heart is unable to pump sufficient blood to meet the metabolic demands of the body, or can do so only at the expense of elevated filling pressures. It represents the end stage of many cardiac diseases and carries significant morbidity and mortality.
treatment_protocols: ICD or CRT device therapy (indicated LVEF ≤35%)

severity_levels.mild: NYHA Class I-II, LVEF 41-49%, symptoms with significant exertion
severity_levels.moderate: NYHA Class III, LVEF 30-40%, symptoms with minimal exertion
severity_levels.severe: NYHA Class IV, LVEF <30%, symptoms at rest, refractory to medical therapy

diseases.name: Acute Pancreatitis
diseases.description: Acute pancreatitis is an inflammatory condition of the pancreas that occurs when pancreatic enzymes become activated within the pancreas itself, causing autodigestion of the gland. It ranges from mild, self-limiting edematous pancreatitis to severe necrotizing disease with multi-organ failure.
common_symptoms: Severe epigastric pain radiating to the back
recommended_tests: Serum lipase (3x upper limit of normal)
treatment_protocols: Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation (Ringer's lactate preferred)
NPO initially, early enteral feeding within 24-48h if tolerated
Cholecystectomy during same admission if biliary pancreatitis
Nutritional support (nasogastric or nasojejunal tube if needed)
Antibiotics only for infected necrosis (not prophylactic)
Endoscopic or percutaneous drainage for walled-off necrosis
ICU admission for severe disease with organ failure

severity_levels.mild: No organ failure, no local/systemic complications, resolves in 48-72 hours
severity_levels.moderate: Transient organ failure (<48h) or local complications
severity_levels.severe: Persistent organ failure (>48h), necrotizing pancreatitis, high mortality risk

diseases.name: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
diseases.description: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) is a chronic autoimmune disease characterized by the production of autoantibodies against nuclear antigens, leading to multisystem inflammation. It predominantly affects women of childbearing age and has a variable clinical course with periods of flare and remission.
treatment_protocols: Hydroxychloroquine (baseline therapy for all SLE patients)
Mycophenolate mofetil or cyclophosphamide for lupus nephritis

severity_levels.mild: Skin and joint involvement only, normal organ function, manageable with hydroxychloroquine and NSAIDs
severity_levels.moderate: Serositis, hematologic involvement, or mild renal disease requiring corticosteroids and immunosuppressants
severity_levels.severe: Lupus nephritis class III/IV, CNS lupus, pulmonary hemorrhage, or catastrophic antiphospholipid syndrome

diseases.name: Lung Adenocarcinoma
diseases.description: Lung adenocarcinoma is the most common histologic subtype of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), arising from glandular cells in the peripheral lung tissue. It is the leading cause of cancer-related death worldwide and is strongly associated with targeted molecular alterations that guide precision therapy.
risk_factors: EGFR mutation (more common in never-smokers, Asian ethnicity)
recommended_tests: Molecular profiling (EGFR, ALK, ROS1, BRAF, KRAS, NTRK, RET, MET)
treatment_protocols: Stage I: Surgical resection (lobectomy) ± adjuvant chemotherapy
Stage II: Surgery + adjuvant chemotherapy ± radiation
Stage IIIA: Multimodal (chemoradiation + immunotherapy or surgery + adjuvant therapy)
PD-L1 ≥50% without driver mutation: Pembrolizumab monotherapy
Radiation therapy for palliation or curative intent (stage III)
Supportive care and palliative care integration

severity_levels.mild: Stage I (tumor ≤4cm, no nodal or distant metastasis), surgical cure possible
severity_levels.moderate: Stage II-IIIA, regional nodal involvement, multimodal therapy required
severity_levels.severe: Stage IIIB-IV, advanced or metastatic disease, palliative/systemic therapy focus

metadata.disclaimer: This knowledge base is for research and educational purposes only. It does not constitute medical advice. Always verify clinical information against current guidelines and institutional protocols.
